evals.prompt: I'm building a tower defense game in Unity. I need a full game spec covering the core tower placement mechanics, a wave management system, an upgrade/progression system, and a simple economy. The game targets mobile (iOS/Android) with a casual audience. Can you create the GDD for me?
evals.expected_output: Creates Docs/Specs/_INDEX.md with game overview, feature map, art/audio direction, platform targets. Creates individual feature spec files: Tower_Placement.md, Wave_Management.md, Upgrade_System.md, Economy.md. Each feature file follows the feature-template.md structure with at least 1 Mermaid diagram. All files pass validation.
expectations: Creates Docs/Specs/_INDEX.md file
Creates at least 4 separate feature spec files in Docs/Specs/
Each feature file has sections 1-6 (Overview through Dependencies)
At least 3 total Mermaid diagrams across all files
_INDEX.md contains a Feature Map table linking to each feature file
No TODO/TBD/FIXME text in any output file
No unfilled {placeholder} text in any output file
validate_spec.py passes on the Docs/Specs/ directory

evals.prompt: I have an existing Unity project with a combat system. The project has scripts in Assets/_Project/Features/Combat/ including DamageCalculator.cs, WeaponController.cs, and ComboSystem.cs. Write a feature spec for just the combat system based on what you find in the codebase.
evals.expected_output: Creates Docs/Specs/Combat_System.md following the feature-template.md. Investigates the codebase first, cites file:line references for existing implementations. Includes architecture class diagram and state diagram for combo system. Dependencies section maps integration points with other systems.
expectations: Creates exactly one file: Docs/Specs/Combat_System.md
File follows the feature-template.md structure
Contains at least 1 Mermaid diagram (class or state diagram)
Cites file:line references from the codebase investigation
Has a Dependencies & Integration section with coupling levels
Overview section has Feature name, Status, and Summary filled
No TODO/TBD/FIXME text
validate_spec.py passes on the file

evals.prompt: The inventory spec at Docs/Specs/Inventory.md is outdated. I added a new crafting subsystem and changed how item stacking works. The ItemStack class now uses a quantity cap from ScriptableObject config instead of hardcoded values. Update the spec to match the current code.
evals.expected_output: Reads existing Docs/Specs/Inventory.md, investigates current codebase for inventory-related changes, produces an updated spec with [UPDATED] tags on changed sections. Preserves user-authored design intent. Updates data model section for new SO-based quantity cap. Adds crafting subsystem to systems design.
expectations: Reads the existing Docs/Specs/Inventory.md before making changes
Investigates the codebase for inventory-related files
Output file contains [UPDATED] tags on changed sections
Preserves sections that haven't changed
Updates the Data Model section to reflect SO-based config
Adds crafting subsystem information to Systems Design
No TODO/TBD/FIXME text
validate_spec.py passes on the updated file
